calls.note: Viewer holds 150 shares at 495 into the demerger; asked if his earlier view had changed, he said for now hold Vedanta.
Very resilient and strong; accumulate slowly only in the dips, no need to chase. A decent buy-and-hold stock - a semiconductor-plus play and also linked to space technologies.
Asked with Inox India for a 12-month horizon; very resilient and strong, buy-and-hold. Accumulate slowly only in the dips; don't chase.
Add slowly in the dips for the long term. Despite the Middle East disruption, management says revenues have no problem and is confident. Stagger entries - if you want 100 shares, add 5-10 at a time.
Asked with L&T for a Rs 25,000 investment; add slowly in the dips for the long term, in a staggered manner rather than in one go.

Agreed with the anchor's 'don't buy IT'. Indian IT went complacent over 3-5 years, fell behind on new technology, neither invented nor acquired, and wasted time on buybacks. They have good cash reserves and can adapt, but have missed the best of times - the next one to two years are crucial. Only consider them after they clearly state their roadmap.
